title: Email notifications arriving hours late
body: Task assignment notifications used to arrive within a minute or two. For the past week, they've been arriving 2-4 hours late. This is causing people to miss deadlines because they don't know they've been assigned a task until hours later. It seems to be worse in the morning — notifications sent around 9-10am take the longest to arrive. Afternoon notifications are usually faster but still delayed.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for the detailed report — the timing pattern you've described (worse in the morning, faster in the afternoon) is really helpful.

Could you walk me through the exact steps to reproduce this? For example:
- How are you assigning the task? (UI, API, bulk assignment, etc.)
- How are you measuring the delay? (Comparing the assignment timestamp in TaskFlow to the email received timestamp?)
- Does this happen for all task types / all projects, or only specific ones?

This will help us narrow down where in the pipeline the delay is occurring.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I'm assigning tasks through the regular UI — just the normal task creation form where you pick the assignee. Nothing fancy, no bulk assignments or API usage. For the delay, yeah I'm basically comparing when I assign the task (I can see the timestamp in TaskFlow) to when the person tells me they got the email. Sometimes I check the email headers too and the send time is hours after I made the assignment. It happens across all our projects as far as I can tell — it's not specific to one project or task type. It really does seem like a system-wide thing that started about a week ago. Before that it was basically instant.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for the clear reproduction details — that's really helpful, especially confirming it's system-wide and started about a week ago.

Could you share some environment details? Specifically:
- What version of TaskFlow are you running (or are you on the cloud-hosted version)?
- What email provider are the notifications going through (e.g., Gmail, Outlook/Exchange, self-hosted SMTP)?
- Did anything change in your setup around the time this started — such as a TaskFlow upgrade, email configuration change, or infrastructure update?

This will help us figure out whether the delay is on the TaskFlow side (job queue, email sending) or the email delivery side.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: We're on TaskFlow v2.3.1. Email goes through our SMTP relay — I'm not sure of the exact details on that, our IT team set it up. As for changes, I didn't change anything on my end, but I think there was a TaskFlow update about a week ago? I remember seeing something about a new daily digest feature in the release notes or maybe an announcement. I'm not sure if that's related but the timing lines up with when the delays started. No email configuration changes that I know of.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Email notifications delayed 2-4 hours since v2.3.1 update (possible daily digest queue interference)","problem":"Task assignment email notifications that previously arrived within 1-2 minutes are now delayed by 2-4 hours. The delay is system-wide across all projects and task types, worse for morning assignments (9-10am), and started approximately one week ago coinciding with a TaskFlow update.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The recently introduced daily digest feature (released ~1 week ago with or near v2.3.1) likely changed the email sending pipeline — possibly routing individual notifications through the same queue or batching mechanism used for digests, or introducing a shared worker pool that prioritizes digest compilation during morning hours, causing immediate notifications to be delayed.","reproduction_steps":["Assign a task to a user via the standard TaskFlow UI task creation form","Note the assignment timestamp shown in TaskFlow","Wait for the assignee to receive the email notification","Compare the TaskFlow assignment timestamp to the email send timestamp in the email headers","Expect to see a 2-4 hour gap, especially for assignments made in the 9-10am window"],"environment":"TaskFlow v2.3.1, email delivered via organization SMTP relay (specific configuration managed by IT), cloud or self-hosted not confirmed but likely self-hosted given SMTP relay setup","severity":"high","impact":"All users across all projects are affected. Delayed notifications cause team members to miss task assignments for hours, leading to missed deadlines and workflow disruption. Morning assignments are most severely impacted.","recommended_fix":"Investigate the email sending pipeline changes introduced alongside the daily digest feature. Specifically: (1) Check whether immediate notification jobs and digest jobs share a queue or worker pool; (2) Look for batching or scheduling logic that may be inadvertently capturing individual notifications; (3) Examine queue depth and processing times, especially during the morning window when digest compilation likely runs; (4) Review the job scheduling configuration for the digest feature to see if it blocks or deprioritizes other email jobs.","proposed_test_case":"Assign a task via the UI and verify that the email notification is sent (check email headers for send timestamp) within 2 minutes of assignment. Run this test both during morning hours (9-10am) and afternoon to confirm consistent delivery times. Additionally, test with the daily digest feature disabled to confirm it as the root cause.","information_gaps":["No server-side logs or email queue metrics to confirm where the delay occurs (TaskFlow job queue vs. SMTP relay)","Exact SMTP relay configuration and whether IT made any changes on that side","Whether the daily digest feature is enabled for the affected users/organization","Exact TaskFlow release that was deployed ~1 week ago (reporter believes v2.3.1 but is not certain of timing)"]}
